items.title: Retired legacy application authorization flow in Gateway ⚡
items.summary: The Gateway fully removes the deprecated ApplicationAuthorization runtime path as part of the credential-as-authorization migration. Obsolete authorization models, lookup helpers, quota tracking logic, and Lua passthrough handling were deleted, while /auth and /record now rely solely on credential-side quota enforcement. The cleanup simplifies request handling, reduces maintenance overhead, and removes unused response metadata without changing production behavior.

items.title: Enhance MCP Slug Handling for Connectors in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the system failed to recognize the correct MCP server identifier, leading to user errors. Key changes include the implementation of a mapping from catalog identifiers to their corresponding MCP slugs, ensuring accurate hints are provided in prompts. Additionally, the loader now accepts catalog identifiers as aliases, improving compatibility and user experience.

items.title: Fix CVM Status Tagging for Accurate Deployment Results in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where the CVM workload provider was incorrectly tagged as 'helm' during deployment status polling. By ensuring that 'workload_provider' is consistently set to 'tencent_cvm' across all relevant functions, the platform will now accurately reflect the deployment status. Eight new unit tests have been added to verify the correct behavior, ensuring reliability in future deployments.

items.title: Enhance Gmail Skill with Null-Guard Fix in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical bug in the Gmail skill where unguarded jq expressions could crash when no messages are returned. The affected recipes have been modified to safely handle empty results by using `.messages // []`. This ensures smoother operation and provides clearer feedback to users when there are no unread emails.

items.title: Automated iOS encryption compliance handling in Nexior 🔐
items.summary: Added the `ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false` flag to the iOS Info.plist so App Store Connect no longer triggers the recurring “Missing Compliance” flow on new uploads. The change reflects Nexior’s use of Apple-provided networking and exempt encryption only. This removes a manual release step for future TestFlight and App Store builds, helping streamline weekly iOS deployments.

items.title: Enhance Credential Handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for consuming Credential-as-Authorization grants from the backend, allowing Nexior to display shared applications without claiming ownership. Key changes include the addition of a 'Shared' badge in the UI and modifications to application loading logic to include granted apps. The implementation is backward-compatible, ensuring existing functionalities remain intact until the backend is fully deployed.

items.title: Revamped Credential Authorization UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Credential-as-Authorization feature, enhancing the application authorization user experience. Users can now opt-in to receive real credentials instead of the legacy quota-based model through a new feature flag. Key changes include a redesigned authorization interface and improved credential management functionalities.

items.title: Enhance ApiUsage with actor_user_id in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `actor_user_id` field to the `ApiUsage` model, allowing for more efficient tracking of API calls made by grantees. The new field is indexed and ensures that grantees can easily retrieve their usage data without scanning the entire metadata. Additionally, the implementation includes thorough tests to validate the new functionality.

items.title: Enhance Feature Flag Handling in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a read-only mirror of the FeatureFlag model in the Gateway, allowing for efficient request-path gating without service boundary crossings. Key additions include async cached lookups and feature flag evaluation helpers, which ensure consistent flag behavior across services. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate the new functionality.

items.title: Streamlined Autologon for Wisdom CVM in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates an autologon feature into the CVM provisioner, ensuring that new Wisdom CVMs boot directly into a functional state without manual intervention. Key changes include the removal of legacy tasks and the introduction of a new scheduled task that runs with the highest privileges, enhancing the user experience and operational efficiency.

items.title: Enhance User Consent Handling and Skill Loader in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the handling of user consent for connected connectors by preventing unnecessary calls to `request_user_consent`, thus improving efficiency. Additionally, the skill loader now accepts namespaced slugs, allowing for better compatibility with future bundled skills. These changes enhance the overall functionality and user experience within the Platform Service.

items.title: Fix namespaced slug emission for active skills in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the `/internal/v1/skills/active` endpoint returns the namespaced identifier for skills installed from the public catalog, resolving issues with the `load_skill` function. The `UserSkill.skill_identifier` is now serialized as `slug` when available, allowing for successful matches with required skills. A new test has been added to verify this functionality.

items.title: Resolve Duplicate Migration Leaves in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update merges two conflicting migration files created for the 0152 leaf-node issue. A new migration file, 0154_merge_duplicate_0153_leaves, has been added to flatten the graph and resolve the conflict. This ensures smooth production rollout and prevents further errors related to conflicting migrations.

items.title: Resolve migration conflict for feature flags and user IDs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a no-op merge migration to resolve a conflict between two migrations, `0152_featureflag` and `0152_actor_user_id`, which both depend on the same previous migration. The new migration ensures that the deployment can proceed without issues, as it touches disjoint schema elements and avoids any data migration risks. The rollout will unblock the current deployment process and stabilize the cluster.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates for Multiple Languages in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for the i18n files from the zh-CN source. Key changes include the addition of new flag files and modifications to existing console files across various languages such as Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese. This enhancement improves localization support and ensures a more inclusive user experience.

items.title: Resolve migration conflicts with a merge in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a no-op merge migration to resolve conflicts caused by two parallel migrations, `0152_actor_user_id` and `0152_featureflag`. The merge ensures that the migration graph has a single leaf, allowing the `check-pr` workflow to pass and restoring the new replicaset to a running state. This change will also create the necessary columns in production, ensuring smooth operation moving forward.

items.title: Fix AuthBackend Endpoint for Aichat2 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the endpoint path for the AuthBackend catalog in the Aichat2 worker, resolving a persistent 404 error in production. The change ensures that the worker can successfully fetch the connector catalog, allowing the consent flow to function properly. Local tests confirm that the worker now receives the expected data structure, enhancing the user experience.

items.title: Empower Admins with Feature Flags Console in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new superuser-only console page for managing feature flags at `/console/admin/feature-flags`. Key features include a real-time rollout slider, scorecard comparisons for on/off cohorts, and evaluation tools for user targeting. The implementation enhances control over feature rollouts, improving the overall user experience and backend integration.

items.title: Update autostart recommendation for Windows in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the documentation in `deploy/windows/run.ps1` to recommend using the ONLOGON/Administrator autostart method instead of the previous SYSTEM/ONSTART approach. The change addresses issues with GUI applications running in session 0, ensuring that WeChat and other applications function correctly by launching in an interactive session. The legacy method remains commented out for reference, while the new documentation highlights potential task cache bugs and links to related resources.

items.title: Streamlined Credential Authorization Process in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Phase 1 of the Credential-as-Authorization redesign, simplifying the authorization model by allowing application owners to mint credentials directly for grantees. Key changes include the addition of `actor_user_id` for tracking API usage by grantees, and new endpoints for managing credentials and applications. Legacy authorization methods remain intact for compatibility during the transition.

items.title: Introducing a Dynamic Feature Flag System in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust database-backed feature flag system to the PlatformBackend, allowing for percentage-based rollouts and kill switches without redeploying code. Key features include a new `FeatureFlag` model, an enhanced `/api/v1/config/` endpoint, and a Django admin interface for real-time flag management. This implementation ensures safer deployments and greater flexibility for operators managing feature access.

items.title: Enhance Consent Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `consentGating` helper to prevent unauthorized skill and MCP server loads in the Platform Service. It ensures that if a requested skill or connector is unconnected, the system will return a structured error and prompt the model to request user consent instead of proceeding with the operation. This change addresses a critical regression identified in previous deployments and enhances overall system reliability.

items.title: Refactor consent tool naming for clarity in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the consent tool from `get_connector_status` to `request_user_consent` across the frontend, aligning with changes made in the PlatformService. Key components such as Message.vue and consentReturn.ts have been updated to reflect this new naming convention. While the wire shape remains unchanged, historic conversations may not match the new tool name, which is an acceptable trade-off for improved clarity.

items.title: Refactor consent handling for connectors in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the `get_connector_status` function to `request_user_consent`, clarifying its purpose to demand user consent rather than simply checking status. The fast path for already-connected capabilities has been removed, ensuring that misuse errors are returned when consent is not properly requested. Additionally, the RULES block has been rewritten to enforce clearer guidelines for connector usage, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: Fix QR Code Rendering Issue in Wisdom 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where the login screen displayed a broken image for the QR code due to missing the `data:image/png;base64,` prefix in the base64 string. The fix ensures that the QR code is correctly rendered by adding the necessary prefix and escaping HTML. This change improves the user experience by enabling WeChat scan-to-login functionality.

items.title: Optimize Replica Count and HPA for Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the baseline replicas from 20 to 5 in the deployment configuration, allowing for more efficient resource usage. Additionally, a new Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) configuration has been added to manage scaling dynamically, enabling the system to handle bursts up to 40 replicas when needed. These changes aim to improve performance while saving CPU and memory resources.

items.title: Optimize CPU Requests for Pay Backend in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: The CPU request for the `pay-backend` service has been reduced from 200m to 50m, aligning better with actual usage of ~3 mcore/pod. This change saves 300m of CPU requests while keeping memory settings unchanged. The overall impact is low risk, as the CPU limit remains at 1 core, allowing for burst handling.

items.title: Optimize CPU Requests for WebExtrator in Web Extrator 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the CPU requests for both the webextrator-api and webextrator-worker deployments based on actual usage metrics. The api's request has been decreased from 250m to 50m, and the worker's from 100m to 50m, leading to a total saving of approximately 400m CPU requests. Memory limits remain unchanged, ensuring stability while optimizing resource usage.

items.title: Optimized CPU Requests for MCP Services in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update reduces the CPU requests for all 15 MCP services from 100m to 30m, aligning with observed usage patterns and Kubernetes recommendations. The change results in a significant saving of 1050m in total CPU requests, freeing up resources for other workloads. The limits remain unchanged, ensuring that services can still handle traffic spikes effectively.

items.title: Fixed COS CDN Cache Headers for Faster Rollouts in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Updated the COS publishing flow to apply explicit Cache-Control headers for deployment artifacts. Rolling `*-latest.zip` builds now refresh at CDN edges within minutes, while immutable versioned archives are cached aggressively for long-term efficiency. The release pipeline also logs applied cache headers and includes cache metadata in the generated manifest to simplify future debugging and verification.

items.title: Enhance Seedance MCP Documentation with Real Test Insights in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new promotional article in Chinese for using Seedance MCP with Claude.ai Web, featuring a hands-on end-to-end test. Key additions include a detailed connector flow, a comprehensive tool list, and a real test showcasing video generation capabilities. The article is designed to guide users through the process with screenshots and FAQs for better understanding.

items.title: Enhance Luma MCP with Claude.ai Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new documentation article detailing the integration of Luma MCP with Claude.ai, showcasing a real end-to-end test. Key features include the addition of Luma as a custom connector via OAuth and confirmation of all eight tools available. A live query demonstrated Luma's capabilities, generating a video in approximately 99 seconds, with the result now accessible online.

items.title: Enhance Claude.ai with Veo MCP for Seamless Video Generation in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new documentation article detailing the integration of Veo MCP with Claude.ai, showcasing a real end-to-end test. Key features include the addition of various tools for video generation and the successful execution of a query that produced a 1080p video in approximately 92 seconds. Screenshots and a video link are provided for reference.

items.title: Revamped Claude.ai ShortURL Guide in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The documentation for the ShortURL MCP has been completely rewritten based on a real end-to-end test with Claude.ai. Key updates include the addition of a custom connector via OAuth and confirmation of four essential tools. The article now features a structured template and includes live session screenshots demonstrating the functionality.

items.title: Claude.ai Serp MCP guide rebuilt around live end-to-end testing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: Reworked the Chinese Serp MCP promotion article using a fully verified Claude.ai web workflow instead of placeholder-style examples. The update documents connector setup, OAuth authorization, all 11 exposed tools, and a real Google search query executed through Claude with validated news results. The article structure was also standardized into a clearer step-by-step format with screenshots and reproducible instructions for live testing.

items.title: Enhance Seedream MCP Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive guide on using Seedream MCP within Claude.ai, featuring a real end-to-end test. The documentation includes detailed steps from setup to generating images with specific prompts, showcasing the integration's capabilities. Key changes include the addition of a new markdown file that provides insights and screenshots of the process.

items.title: Enhance NanoBanana Integration with Claude.ai in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive guide on using NanoBanana MCP within Claude.ai, featuring a real end-to-end test. The documentation includes detailed steps, screenshots, and a successful image generation prompt, showcasing the seamless integration and performance of the service. Users can now easily generate images directly from Claude.ai, enhancing their creative workflows.

items.title: Enhance Midjourney Integration with Claude.ai Walkthrough in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive guide on using Midjourney MCP within Claude.ai, documented in a new article. The walkthrough details the entire process from setup to generating high-quality images, showcasing a successful test that produced a 2×2 grid image. Key features include automatic prompt handling and image generation, enhancing user experience significantly.

items.title: Revamp of Suno × Claude Article with Real Testing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The Suno × Claude article has been completely rewritten to include a real end-to-end walkthrough of the integration process on Claude.ai. Key updates include new URLs, a detailed description of the tool permissions, and a disclaimer about the temporary nature of generated audio links. Enhanced screenshots have also been added to provide a clearer understanding of the user experience.

items.title: Clean up legacy catalog_identifier in Nexior 🔧
items.summary: This update removes the deprecated `catalog_identifier` alias from the `IUserConnectionSummary` interface and related components. The code has been streamlined to ensure only `connector_identifier` is used, enhancing clarity and maintainability. All tests have passed successfully, confirming no runtime behavior changes.

items.title: Remove legacy catalog_identifier alias and dead fallbacks in Frontend Web 🧹
items.summary: Drops the deprecated catalog_identifier alias from connection and skill TypeScript interfaces following backend changes. Cleans up dead fallback logic in Connections.vue that could no longer be triggered. Updates related comments to reflect the finalized API contract. This is a pure cleanup with no runtime behavior changes.

items.title: Auto-launch WeChat on Startup for Seamless Experience in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature that automatically launches WeChat if it is not already running when Wisdom starts. The implementation includes a helper function that checks for WeChat's status and initiates it if necessary, ensuring that users have a smooth experience without manual logon. Safety measures are in place to handle scenarios where WeChat is not found or fails to launch, preventing crashes during startup.

items.title: Fix connection and skill operators for seamless access in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update repoints the connection and skill operators to their new API endpoints, resolving critical production issues. The changes ensure that the Connector tab and Skills section are fully functional again, restoring access to essential features without altering existing page behavior. All endpoints have been verified live to confirm successful integration.

items.title: Streamline runner by removing unnecessary flag in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the `run.ps1` script by eliminating the `-SkipBootstrap` flag, simplifying the boot process. The runner will now always execute the full flow, ensuring that new code changes are applied without the need for re-baking the image. This change enhances efficiency and maintains the public interface, while also keeping essential functions from previous updates.

items.title: Hotfixes oversized JWT token storage to restore admin logins in Backend 🚑
items.summary: This update fixes a production login outage caused by JWT access and refresh tokens exceeding the legacy 3000-character database limit after expanded permission codenames increased token size. The AuthToken model now stores tokens as TextField values, removing the artificial cap without changing API behavior or authentication flows. A lightweight Django migration updates the schema safely on PostgreSQL and prevents future regressions as permission counts grow.

items.title: Cleaned Up Broken AI Crawler References and Dead Assets in Docs 🧹
items.summary: Removed stale and fabricated entries from llms.txt and llms-full.txt after earlier content deletions left dozens of broken URLs behind. The update also deletes an unused api-key-bridge.js asset that was no longer referenced anywhere in the docs configuration. All remaining URLs were validated against existing .mdx files, bringing both crawler-facing indexes to zero broken links and improving documentation integrity for AI search tools and developers.

items.title: Completed the catalog-to-directory rename in Backend 🧹
items.summary: Renamed legacy catalog models, tables, modules, serializers, views, commands, and URLs to resource-typed Connector, Skill, and UserSkill names. Removed backward-compatible alias routes and serializer dual-fields, with a single reversible migration backfilling user skill sources from catalog to installed. Tests were updated around the new names and canonical paths, with obsolete alias and dual-field coverage removed.

items.title: Standardize Chinese Locale Folders in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update normalizes the Chinese locale folders to align with Mintlify's BCP-47 standards. The previous 'cn' folder is now 'zh-Hans', and 'zh-TW' has been renamed to 'zh-Hant'. All relevant content has been moved into their respective folders, ensuring consistency across the documentation.

items.title: Prepared the 3.35.3 mobile release rollout in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Updated Nexior to version 3.35.3 across package metadata and regenerated the lockfile for release consistency. The release shell now aligns iOS and Android version identifiers with the live 3.35.3 web bundle, preparing builds for TestFlight and the Play internal track. This keeps native release pipelines synchronized ahead of deployment.

items.title: Windows startup now finds preinstalled Python and skips unnecessary setup in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Updated the Windows runner to detect preinstalled Python from common install locations before attempting any downloads, preventing startup hangs on baked images. Added readiness checks for Wisdom and its dependencies plus a SkipBootstrap mode that can launch directly when the environment is already prepared. This speeds up autostart on pre-baked Windows images while preserving existing bootstrap behavior for fresh installations and development environments.

items.title: Enhance API Security with Token Requirement in Wisdom 🔒
items.summary: This update enforces API token authentication for all `/api/*` routes when the `API_TOKEN` environment variable is set. The implementation includes support for both Bearer token headers and query parameters, ensuring secure access to the API. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the authentication mechanism.

items.title: Enhance OTA Upload Speed with Global Acceleration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the COS global acceleration endpoint to improve upload speeds from GitHub Actions to the ap-beijing region. By routing uploads through Tencent's global edge, we significantly reduce timeout issues, enhancing the reliability of the publish-ota process. The necessary changes have been made to the client configuration and a new patch file has been added.

items.title: Refactor consent handling with new connector identifiers in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the consent handling to use the new `connector_identifier` and updates the related URL paths to `/connectors/`. Key changes include modifications to the `ConnectorConsentCard` and `connectorCatalogCache` to support both the new and legacy identifiers during the transition. The implementation ensures backward compatibility while preparing for future cleanup in AuthBackend.

items.title: Shared types updated for connector and skill identifier rollout in Common Frontend 🔄
items.summary: Shared frontend types now support the platform-wide catalog-to-connector/skill rename rollout by introducing canonical `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier` fields alongside deprecated `catalog_identifier` aliases. The update also refreshes connector catalog documentation to reference the new `/connectors/` API alias while preserving compatibility with legacy endpoints. These are type-only changes with no runtime behavior impact, allowing downstream clients to safely migrate during the staged backend rollout.

items.title: Connector & Skill Identifier Rollout Lands in Frontend Web 🔄
items.summary: Updated connection and skill operator types to adopt the new canonical `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier` fields while preserving backward compatibility with legacy `catalog_identifier` aliases. The Connections view now safely joins against either backend schema version, keeping connector metadata and permissions rendering stable throughout the staged rollout. Documentation comments and API references were also refreshed to align with the new `/connectors/` naming.

items.title: Connector Identifier Rollout Lands Across AI Chat Connection Flows in Platform Service 🔄
items.summary: This update completes the Stage 2 client-side migration from `catalog_identifier` to `connector_identifier` across AI chat connection handling, prompts, credential flows, and connector status checks. Matching logic now prioritizes the new field while preserving backward compatibility with legacy payloads during the rollout window. Tests were updated to validate both canonical and fallback paths, helping ensure safe deployment alongside the AuthBackend transition.

items.title: Refactor Connections: Rename Catalog Identifiers and Introduce New URL Aliases in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This PR marks the first stage of a refactor to eliminate 'catalog' references in the AuthBackend. Key changes include renaming `catalog_identifier` columns to `connector_identifier` and `skill_identifier`, along with the introduction of new URL aliases for connectors and installed skills. Backwards compatibility is maintained through read-only aliases and dual-write mechanisms to ensure seamless integration for existing frontends.

items.title: Verified OpenAI MCP stays fully aligned with latest API docs in M C Ps ✅
items.summary: This update audited the openai MCP server against the latest OpenAPI documentation and confirmed full parity across supported endpoints, model enums, and request schema coverage. Checks covered chat completions, embeddings, image generation and edits, responses, and tasks APIs. No functional drift or missing schema mappings were identified, so no code changes were required.

items.title: Decoupling Docs from PlatformBackend & Revamping Structure in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully disconnects the Mintlify documentation site from the PlatformBackend sync, allowing for direct edits within the repository. It also introduces a restructured information architecture, enhancing user navigation with new tabs for Pricing, FAQ, and About, while adding several new pages to improve content accessibility and coherence.

items.title: Documentation sync for overview updates in M C Ps 📚
items.summary: This update addresses a sync issue triggered by changes flagged in the upstream documentation for the 'overview' section. Since there are no corresponding MCP servers or relevant entries in this monorepo, the changes are purely documentation-related, such as navigation adjustments. No code changes were necessary as the repository remains in sync.

items.title: Fix(mobile): add top safe-area padding so content clears iOS Dynamic Island / Android status bar in Nexior 🐛
Fix(ota): COS bucket region is ap-beijing, not ap-hongkong in Nexior 🐛
Fix(wisdom): emit cloudbase-init magic header so UserData actually runs in Backend 🐛
Ci(ota): publish-ota workflow + helper script for COS bundle releases in Nexior 🧪
Ship OTA live updates via Capgo with COS-hosted bundles in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Integrates @capgo/capacitor-updater to enable OTA delivery of JS/HTML/CSS bundles from a COS-hosted manifest, allowing hotfixes without app store releases. Adds a lightweight live update wrapper that runs fire-and-forget after the version gate to avoid blocking app startup. Updates Capacitor config, dependencies, and app bootstrap to support safe, feature-flagged update checks with rollback-safe behavior. Expands mobile release docs with a full OTA workflow and rollout guidance.

items.title: Implement Version Gate for Native Apps in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a version gate for the native iOS and Android applications, enhancing user experience during app startup. Depending on the installed version, users may encounter a hard block or a soft prompt to update, ensuring they are always using a supported version. The implementation includes new logic for version comparison and localized messages for various languages.

items.title: Documentation sync for overview updates in M C Ps 📚
items.summary: This update reflects a no-op sync triggered by upstream documentation changes for the `overview` service, including zh-CN translations and a DeepSeek navigation adjustment. As `overview` is a documentation-only service without an OpenAPI spec or corresponding MCP server, no code changes were necessary. The monorepo is now fully in sync with the latest documentation.

items.title: Enhance Suno Docs with Parameter Ranges in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the documentation for the Suno provider by clearly defining the valid ranges for the parameters `weirdness`, `style_influence`, and `audio_weight` as 0-1. The changes include updated descriptions in both English and Chinese documentation, as well as modifications to the OpenAPI schema to enforce these constraints. This ensures that SDK/UI integrators, including our Nexior frontend, can avoid validation errors when using these parameters.

items.title: Scale Advanced Sliders to 0-1 for Suno and Producer in Nexior 🎛️
items.summary: This update adjusts the advanced sliders for 'weirdness', 'style influence', and 'audio weight' in the Suno and Producer panels to a range of 0-1, aligning with the upstream API requirements. A migration script has been added to correct any persisted values greater than 1, ensuring a smooth transition for existing users. Additionally, all internationalization descriptions have been updated to reflect the new range.

items.title: New mobile app version endpoint added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced a new public endpoint, `GET /api/v1/app-version/`, which allows mobile clients to check for the latest and minimum supported app versions. This feature is crucial for managing client upgrades and ensuring compatibility with backend changes. The endpoint is unauthenticated and can be easily configured via environment variables.

items.title: Fix .env loading to ensure API_TOKEN availability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the configuration to load the `.env` file from the install root instead of the current working directory. This change ensures that the `API_TOKEN` is correctly read regardless of how the Wisdom application is launched, preventing bearer authentication from being disabled. This fix is crucial for the upcoming migration that renames the auto-provisioned key, enhancing security for tenant CVMs.

items.title: Update wisdom deployment and fix API token issue in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new migration that swaps the wisdom service deployment to the `img-eyraq5nz` image, which includes WeChat pre-installed and system Python 3.11. Additionally, the environment variable name has been corrected from `WISDOM_API_TOKEN` to `API_TOKEN`, resolving a silent authentication bug that affected bearer auth on tenant CVMs. These changes enhance the deployment process and ensure proper authentication functionality.

items.title: Enhance Windows Autostart for Seamless Boot in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Windows autostart command in `run.ps1` to trigger at system boot rather than user logon. By using `/SC ONSTART` and running as `NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM`, the task ensures that Wisdom starts automatically without requiring user login, enhancing deployment efficiency. Additionally, a `/Query` example has been included for easier task inspection.

items.title: Streamline Windows Deployment by Using System Python Directly in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the Windows deployment process by eliminating the creation of a virtual environment, which previously added unnecessary overhead. Now, dependencies are installed directly using the system Python 3.11, reducing boot time and simplifying the installation phase. The changes enhance efficiency and clarity for users running the deployment script.

items.title: Restore Python 3.8 Compatibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a future import to the auth module, ensuring compatibility with Python 3.8 by preventing the evaluation of PEP 604 union types at import time. The change addresses a critical issue that caused deployment failures and ensures that future rollouts of the auth-backend will proceed smoothly. All tests remain unaffected, maintaining stability in production.

items.title: Fix Windows PowerShell 5.1 parsing in deploy script and ASCII compatibility 🛠️ in Wisdom 🛠️
items.summary: This update resolves a PowerShell 5.1 parsing failure caused by Unicode dashes being misread under non-UTF-8 code pages. The script is now fully ASCII to ensure consistent parsing across Windows environments, including fresh installations. It also improves safety by renaming a shadowed automatic variable and adding a TLS 1.2 fallback for older .NET setups.

items.title: Unified OAuth Connection Naming Across Worker Flows in Platform Service 🔄
items.summary: Updated worker-side connection and auth-mode vocabulary to align with AuthBackend and AuthFrontend, replacing legacy values with oauth_dcr, oauth_static, byoc, and public. Implicit connection handling now routes through public, while MCP and OAuth logic were updated to recognize the new identifiers consistently. Test fixtures and validation coverage were refreshed to match the renamed models, with all TypeScript checks and test suites passing.

items.title: Align OAuth connection modes with AuthBackend vocabulary in Frontend Web 🔄
items.summary: Updated AuthFrontend to use the same shared connection-kind and auth-mode values as AuthBackend and aichat2-worker. The refactor replaces legacy values like `custom_oauth` and `oauth_preset` with `oauth_dcr`, `oauth_static`, `byoc`, and `public`, while removing `acedata_token` entirely. Connection filtering logic, inline auth handling, and related UI comments were refreshed to match the new platform-wide naming scheme. TypeScript and ESLint validation both completed cleanly.

items.title: Unified OAuth and Connection Modes Across Install Flows in Backend 🔄
items.summary: Standardized connection and connector auth enums around a shared vocabulary: oauth_dcr, oauth_static, byoc, and public, while removing the unused acedata_token path. Updated install flows, adapters, serializers, seeds, migrations, and tests to align with the new model and renamed the custom OAuth adapter stack to oauth_dcr. The migration safely remaps existing connection data and ensures downstream services can rely on consistent auth mode semantics during authorization and install handling.

items.title: Collapsed Windows Bootstrap Into One Re-runnable Script in Wisdom ⚡
items.summary: The Windows deployment flow was simplified into a single `run.ps1` entrypoint that installs Python if needed, downloads the latest release, preserves existing `.env` files, sets up dependencies, and launches Wisdom directly. Legacy bootstrap wrappers, scheduled-task registration logic, marker files, and README instructions were removed to reduce operational complexity. The new approach is deterministic and re-runnable, with autostart setup now handled separately through an explicit one-time `schtasks` command.

items.title: URL-Driven Auth App Gating Lands in Frontend Web 🚦
items.summary: Application Authorization UI access now uses the sticky `?features=authapp` URL flag instead of relying on backend config checks. The update wires both the application list and authorization views into the shared frontend feature-flag utility, matching the existing `sites` rollout behavior with cookie persistence and opt-in controls. Backend authorization enforcement remains unchanged, while the frontend gains safer beta rollout and rollback flexibility without requiring redeploys.

items.title: Windows bootstrap automation lands for custom image deployments in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Added a new Windows deployment bootstrap flow for custom images that downloads the latest Wisdom release from the CDN, installs it to C:\\wisdom, configures autostart, and launches the service automatically. The new PowerShell and batch bootstrap scripts support idempotent reruns, scheduled task self-healing, detailed bootstrap logging, and cloudbase-init integration. The flow also preserves externally provided .env configuration values, ensuring existing deployment token and environment precedence behavior remains intact.

items.title: Windows One-Click Release Setup Lands in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: Added a full Windows-friendly install and launch flow for the Wisdom release zip, including new PowerShell scripts and double-clickable .bat shims for install, start, and stop operations. The release packaging workflow now ships all required scripts, configs, and helper files so end users can set up a local .venv and launch the server without manual CLI steps. README documentation was expanded with a guided quick-start flow for downloading, installing, and running Wisdom on Windows.

items.title: Application Authorization UI Opens Up for All Users in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The Application Authorization UI is now available to all users whenever the backend ENABLE_APPLICATION_AUTHORIZATION feature flag is enabled. This removes the previous superuser-only restriction so grantees can access the “Authorized to Me” tab and discover shared applications. Existing backend feature-flag gating and API-level access enforcement remain unchanged.
